name: Example Steve
subtitle: Demo character - not real data
keyQuestion: What would I do if I knew I couldn't fail?
claudeRead: Example Steve is in a genuinely transitional moment - the career search is real now, not theoretical, and the side project session from last week is the clearest evidence yet that the right direction produces a qualitatively different kind of engagement. The main risk is that financial pressure shortens the search before the right thing is found. THE INNER CRITIC is active but being noticed more quickly. The body is coming online - three months smoke-free and running consistently. Rest is the most significant unmet need and the one most likely to quietly undermine everything else if it stays unaddressed.

allies.name: Jordan
allies.desc: Best friend since university. Calls me out gently and shows up consistently.
allies.why: Knows the full version of me - the messy bits included. Rare.

allies.name: Dog
allies.desc: Unconditional support. Not always the most talkative, but always in my corner.

mainQuest.title: Build a Career I Actually Care About
mainQuest.description: Move from doing work that pays the bills to work that uses my real strengths and feels worth getting up for.
mainQuest.whyItMatters: I spend more waking hours at work than anywhere else. If it's hollow, everything else feels thin too.
mainQuest.doneWhen: Earning a sustainable income doing work I would choose even if I didn't need the money.

nextSteps.text: Identify three roles that match my strengths and write down why each one excites me
Have one honest conversation with someone already doing work I admire
Spend two hours this week on a side project that feels genuinely mine

sliderLabels.air: More aware than acting - noticing the patterns without changing them yet
sliderLabels.fire: Motivation patchy - flickers of purpose but mostly running on obligation
sliderLabels.water: Some aliveness - running and cooking are reliable, rest of week flat
sliderLabels.earth: Reasonably grounded - self-criticism present but not dominating

enemies.name: PROCRASTINATION
enemies.desc: Kicks in whenever a task feels ambiguous or high-stakes. The gap between starting and not starting is enormous.

enemies.name: SELF-DOUBT
enemies.desc: Quiet voice that says others are better qualified, more talented, more deserving. Loudest just before any real attempt.

enemies.name: COMPARISON TRAP
enemies.desc: Scrolling through others' curated success and measuring my messy middle against their highlight reel.

limitingBeliefs.title: THE IMPOSTER
limitingBeliefs.belief: "I'm not smart or talented enough to do the work I actually want to do."
limitingBeliefs.desc: Shows up most strongly when applying for jobs or sharing creative work publicly. Reframes ordinary learning curves as proof of incompetence.

limitingBeliefs.title: THE PERFECTIONIST
limitingBeliefs.belief: "If I can't do it perfectly, there's no point starting."
limitingBeliefs.desc: The main engine of procrastination. Creates an impossible standard that ensures nothing ships and nothing gets better.

limitingBeliefs.title: THE PESSIMIST
limitingBeliefs.belief: "Things rarely work out for me the way they do for other people."
limitingBeliefs.desc: Selective memory that catalogues failures and dismisses wins. Makes risk feel uniquely dangerous rather than ordinary.

empoweringBeliefs.title: THE PROBLEM SOLVER
empoweringBeliefs.belief: "I can figure out hard things if I stay in the room long enough."
empoweringBeliefs.desc: Track record of working through ambiguous problems is real. The evidence is there when I look for it.

empoweringBeliefs.title: THE LONG GAME
empoweringBeliefs.belief: "Slow progress in the right direction beats fast progress in the wrong one."
empoweringBeliefs.desc: Useful counter to urgency and comparison. The career search isn't behind schedule - it's just thorough.

empoweringBeliefs.title: THE BODY KNOWS
empoweringBeliefs.belief: "The aliveness I feel when working on the right thing is data, not coincidence."
empoweringBeliefs.desc: The qualitative difference between the side project and the day job is the clearest signal available. Trust it.

skills.name: Set Boundaries
skills.description: Say no to requests that drain energy or conflict with priorities without over-explaining or apologising.
skills.whyItMatters: Without boundaries, the career I want stays theoretical — time and energy get consumed by everyone else's agenda.

skills.name: Ask For Help
skills.description: Reach out before stuck, not after. Let people contribute rather than white-knuckling through alone.
skills.whyItMatters: The Imposter says asking reveals weakness. The opposite is true — it's how fast progress actually happens.

skills.name: Stay Consistent
skills.description: Show up to the important habits even when motivation is absent. The feeling follows the action, not the other way around.
skills.whyItMatters: Running, reading, and the side project all depend on this. Consistency is the compound interest of behaviour.

skills.name: Say No
skills.description: Decline social or work obligations that conflict with recovery, focus time, or actual priorities.
skills.whyItMatters: Every yes to the wrong thing is a no to something that actually matters. This is a prerequisite for the main quest.

skills.name: Problem Solving
skills.description: Break complex or ambiguous problems into clear components and find a workable path through them.
skills.whyItMatters: Almost every role I want requires this. It is the capability that earns trust fastest.

skills.name: Written Communication
skills.description: Express ideas clearly and compellingly in writing - proposals, emails, explanations - without over-engineering.
skills.whyItMatters: Writing is thinking made visible. In remote or async work it is the primary signal of competence.

skills.name: Empathy
skills.description: Read what someone actually needs underneath what they say, and respond to that rather than the surface.
skills.whyItMatters: The foundation of every good collaboration, and the thing that turns skill into genuine influence.

sideQuests.title: Start Running
sideQuests.nextStep: Run three times this week, no distance requirement - just show up
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Physical health is the foundation everything else is built on. When I run consistently, everything else is easier.

sideQuests.title: Build the Portfolio
sideQuests.nextStep: Publish two case studies by end of March
sideQuests.whyItMatters: The career shift needs proof of work, not just intent. This is the thing that makes it real to anyone else.

sideQuests.title: Read 12 Books This Year
sideQuests.nextStep: Finish the current book before starting anything else
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Deep reading slows me down in a useful way and gives me ideas I could not generate alone.

sideQuests.title: Declutter Home
sideQuests.nextStep: Clear the spare room - it is the last section and it has been sitting for a month
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Environment shapes thinking. A clean space is a clear head.

sideQuests.title: Learn Spanish
sideQuests.nextStep: Complete one Duolingo lesson every day for two weeks straight
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Opens up travel, connects with family heritage, and is one thing that is genuinely just for me.

completedQuests.title: Quit Smoking
completedQuests.howResolved: Cold turkey after a chest infection made the cost undeniable. Three months clean.

drains.name: Late-night scrolling
drains.desc: Reliably ends with feeling worse. Eats into sleep and replaces rest with passive consumption.

drains.name: Saying yes to avoid discomfort
drains.desc: Fills the calendar with obligations that don't align with priorities. Makes the main quest feel further away.

drains.name: Skipping the gym when stressed
drains.desc: The moment it would help most is exactly when it gets cut. Reinforces the pattern of deprioritising health under pressure.

flowSources.name: Running
flowSources.desc: First 10 minutes are hard, then the thinking quiets down. Best problem-solving happens here.

flowSources.name: Cooking
flowSources.desc: Hands busy, mind relaxed. The one hour a day that feels genuinely restorative.

flowSources.name: Music
flowSources.desc: Playing guitar alone — no audience, no performance. One of the few fully unproductive activities that still feels worthwhile.

flowSources.name: Time with family
flowSources.desc: Sunday dinners where nothing is at stake. Laughter that doesn't require effort.

flowSources.name: Reading
flowSources.desc: Long-form nonfiction, late at night. The world shrinks to the page.

needs.name: Connection
needs.description: Genuine closeness with people who know the real version, not the managed one. Patchy — good with a few people, absent with most.

needs.name: Rest
needs.description: Recovery time that isn't immediately colonised by guilt or a to-do list. Currently a significant deficit.

needs.name: Autonomy
needs.description: Control over how time and energy are spent. Better than it was — working from home has helped.

needs.name: Recognition
needs.description: Feeling that effort and contribution are seen and valued. Work environment is neutral at best on this.

values.name: Authenticity
values.description: Living in line with what I actually think and feel, not what looks right from the outside.

values.name: Family
values.description: Being present and reliable for the people who matter most. The anchor when everything else is uncertain.

values.name: Health
values.description: Physical and mental wellbeing as a non-negotiable foundation, not a reward for good behaviour.

values.name: Growth
values.description: Getting better at things that matter, especially interpersonal and professional skills.

values.name: Financial Security
values.description: Enough stability to make genuine choices — not wealth for its own sake, but freedom from scarcity anxiety.

entries.title: Two Hours That Felt Like Mine
events: Blocked out Saturday morning for the side project - phone in another room
Worked for two uninterrupted hours on the portfolio site for the first time
Jordan called in the evening, told him what I had been working on
Said no to a colleague asking for help with their presentation on Friday
insights: The two hours felt different to normal work - less like performance, more like contact with something real
Telling Jordan about the project made it feel more real, not more exposed
Saying no to the colleague was uncomfortable for about thirty seconds and then fine
PROCRASTINATION didn't fire once during the session - having a specific small task was the difference
tensions: Whether the side project is the actual direction or just a way of feeling busy without risking the real thing
The session was good but one session is not a pattern - Stay Consistent is still unproven here
linkedQuests: Build a Career I Actually Care About

entries.title: The Coffee That Changed the Frame
events: Met Priya for coffee - she made the career switch two years ago and is doing work I find genuinely interesting
She asked what I was actually good at, not what I wanted to do - different question
Spent the afternoon writing the first honest version of the strengths list
Skipped the gym to finish the list, slept badly
insights: SELF-DOUBT reframed Priya's question as a test I was about to fail - I noticed it this time
The honest strengths list was harder to write than the aspirational one, and more useful
Skipping the gym to finish something is a pattern worth watching - Health as a value is inconsistent here
Priya said the transition took longer than expected but she never questioned whether it was right - that landed
tensions: Whether I am choosing roles that actually fit or roles that look like the right answer from the outside
Financial pressure is shortening the timeline in ways that might distort the search
linkedQuests: Build a Career I Actually Care About

entries.title: The List That Actually Felt Honest
events: Wrote down three roles that genuinely excite me - first time doing it without immediately dismissing each one
Read for 45 minutes before bed, finished the current book
Mum called, told her about the career thinking - she listened more than advised this time
Sat with the Comparison Trap for a while after seeing a former colleague announce a promotion
insights: Writing the list without dismissing each entry required noticing THE INNER CRITIC in real time and continuing anyway
The promotion announcement triggered COMPARISON TRAP immediately and reliably - the pattern is faster than thought
Mum was more useful when I asked her to listen rather than advise - that is a version of Ask For Help that actually worked
Finishing the book felt like a small win that cost nothing - the Read 12 Books side quest is moving without effort
tensions: The three roles on the list are exciting but all feel slightly out of reach - is that realistic assessment or THE INNER CRITIC curating ambition downward
Comparison Trap was strong today - worth watching whether it intensifies as the career search becomes more real and visible
linkedQuests: Build a Career I Actually Care About

entries.title: Three Months Clean
events: Three months without a cigarette - marked it quietly, told Jordan
Ran twice this week, both times over 20 minutes
Cleared the living room and bedroom sections of the flat
Spent the evening reading rather than watching anything
insights: The chest infection was the forcing function, but staying stopped has required something else - a quiet decision to stop negotiating with the habit
Quitting smoking and starting to run arrived at the same time - the body responding to being taken seriously
THE INNER CRITIC tried to minimise the three months: said it does not count because the trigger was external. That is exactly what it does.
Telling Jordan felt important - externalising the milestone made it more real and harder to quietly erase
tensions: Whether the motivation to run holds once it stops feeling like a reaction to quitting smoking
Rest is still a deficit - evenings feel productive but not genuinely restorative

entries.title: The Declutter That Was Also Something Else
events: Cleared the kitchen, hallway, and most of the bedroom over a long weekend
Threw out three bin bags of things kept for reasons that no longer made sense
Set up a proper workspace in the corner of the living room
Cooked a real meal for the first time in about two weeks
insights: Clearing the physical space had an immediate effect on the mental noise - not metaphorical, genuinely quieter
Most of what I threw away had been kept out of vague obligation, not actual use or attachment
The workspace setup felt like a statement of intent - this is where the career work will happen
Cooking rather than ordering felt like the same signal as the declutter: taking my own context seriously
tensions: The spare room is still untouched - it has become the thing I keep not doing, which probably means something
The workspace is set up but Deep Work sessions are still short and interrupted - the environment is ready but the habit is not
linkedQuests: Build a Career I Actually Care About

boss.name: THE INNER CRITIC
boss.desc: The central voice that frames every setback as evidence of fundamental inadequacy. Feels like a fair assessment. It isn't.
boss.whyBoss: Every other enemy is this one in disguise — Procrastination is the Critic saying you'll fail, Self-Doubt is the Critic questioning your worth, the Comparison Trap is the Critic finding external evidence.

currentEnemies.name: PROCRASTINATION
currentEnemies.desc: Kicks in whenever a task feels ambiguous or high-stakes. The gap between starting and not starting is enormous. Masquerades as preparation.
currentEnemies.trigger: Ambiguous or important tasks with no clear first step
currentEnemies.shortTermBenefit: Protects against the pain of trying and failing. Feels like caution, not avoidance.
currentEnemies.origin: Likely formed around high-stakes school performance where effort without success felt worse than not trying at all.

currentEnemies.name: SELF-DOUBT
currentEnemies.desc: Quiet voice that says others are better qualified, more talented, more deserving. Loudest just before any real attempt. Speaks in first person so it sounds like truth.
currentEnemies.shortTermBenefit: Keeps expectations low so disappointment hurts less. Feels like realism.
currentEnemies.origin: Unknown - may relate to early environments where confidence was penalised or not modelled. Worth exploring.

currentEnemies.name: COMPARISON TRAP
currentEnemies.desc: Scrolling through others' curated success and measuring a messy middle against their highlight reel. Always loses because the comparison is structurally unfair.
currentEnemies.trigger: Social media, professional updates, seeing peers succeed
currentEnemies.shortTermBenefit: Creates a temporary sense of direction - if others have succeeded this way, the path must be real.
currentEnemies.origin: Unknown - the specific conditions that made external benchmarks feel necessary are not yet clear.

graveyard.name: SUNDAY DREAD
graveyard.howDefeated: Lost its footing when the job ended. Without the thing it was anticipating, it had nothing to attach to.

graveyard.name: THE AVOIDER
graveyard.howDefeated: Named it properly for the first time in therapy. Avoidance disguised as being busy. Once it had a name it stopped working as cover.

graveyard.name: INSTAGRAM SPIRAL
graveyard.howDefeated: Deleted the app after noticing the pattern: open it feeling okay, close it feeling worse, every time. Not willpower - just removing the trigger.

activities.name: Deep Work
activities.description: Focused time on the career transition - research, applications, side project, skill-building. No multitasking, phone away.
activities.whyItMatters: The career I want won't build itself in the margins. It needs protected focused time.

activities.name: Morning Run
activities.whyItMatters: Movement sets the tone for the whole day. Miss it and everything feels harder.

activities.name: Gym Sessions
activities.description: Strength training. Three or four times a week is the target.
activities.whyItMatters: Weekly consistency matters more than individual sessions.

achievements.title: Smoke Free
achievements.description: Three months without a cigarette after a decade of trying. The chest infection was the push - staying stopped is the achievement.

achievements.title: The Declutterer
achievements.description: Cleared 75% of the home in a single sustained push. Turns out the clutter was also mental.

achievements.title: Signal Over Noise
achievements.description: Deleted Instagram after noticing the pattern: open it feeling okay, close it feeling worse, every time.

classes.title: Creator
classes.description: Drawn to making things - music, cooking, projects. Finds meaning in the act of creation itself, not just the result. The side project is not a distraction; it might be the point.

classes.title: Achiever
classes.description: Goal-oriented and persistent when the goal actually matters. The risk is chasing achievement as validation rather than genuine progress. Learning to tell the difference.
